Title: Deciphering phenylalanine-derived salicylic acid biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase, clade V; contains C-terminal PTS1 peroxisomal targeting signal
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Dedicated terminal SA biosynthetic module downstream of peroxisomal beta-oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream step of the BEBT-BBH-BSE module; downstream of OsCNL-mediated cinnamoyl-CoA formation and beta-oxidation to benzoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the benzoyl group from benzoyl-CoA to benzyl alcohol to form benzylbenzoate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct committed intermediate upstream of salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key dedicated enzyme essential for SA biosynthesis; bottleneck/critical control point supported genetically, not explicitly termed rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated by SA-inducing stimuli; co-expressed with OsCNL1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen induced; responsive to SA-inducing stimuli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central regulator of plant immunity; also involved in abiotic stress response, development and root microbiome assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes salicylic acid accumulation; loss of OsBEBT causes near-complete SA deficiency and reduced disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR frameshift mutants; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; subcellular localization by YFP co-localization; chemical complementation with benzylbenzoate; isotope tracing from labelled BA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling leaves under normal growth; exogenous BA or benzylbenzoate feeding; pathogen infection by Magnaporthe oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used in transient expression/localization assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene co-expression analysis using ATTED-II public RNA-seq/microarray data; pathogen-responsive expression datasets in Extended Data Fig. 10 from NCBI SRA BioProjects (specific accessions not listed in provided text)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBEBT was identified by OsCNL1-based co-expression analysis as a peroxisomal BAHD acyltransferase candidate. 2. Recombinant OsBEBT preferentially used benzoyl-CoA and benzyl alcohol to produce benzylbenzoate in vitro. 3. bebt mutants showed near-complete loss of SA, accumulation of benzoyl-CoA, and loss of benzylbenzoate in vivo. 4. Exogenous benzylbenzoate restored SA levels in bebt and cnl mutants, placing OsBEBT upstream of SA formation. 5. bebt mutants were more susceptible to rice blast, linking OsBEBT-mediated SA biosynthesis to immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports revision of the historical BA-to-SA direct hydroxylation model; BA contributes upstream by conversion back to benzoyl-CoA rather than serving as direct precursor. Functional homologues discussed in Arabidopsis, tobacco, tomato, wheat and cotton.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth defects reported in bebt mutants despite strong SA deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A specific SA biosynthetic enzyme with minimal visible growth penalty, making it a promising target for engineering or selection of disease resistance; may be useful in stacking with BBH and BSE

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase with N-terminal signal peptide for endomembrane system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream step of BEBT-BBH-BSE module; downstream of benzylbenzoate and upstream of benzylsalicylate hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates benzylbenzoate to generate benzylsalicylate in an NADPH-dependent monooxygenase reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate upstream intermediate to SA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key dedicated CYP hydroxylase essential for SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with OsCNL1; pathogen induced as part of conserved module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen induced; co-expression with OsCNL1 supports coordinated SA biosynthetic regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central regulator of plant immunity; also involved in abiotic stress response, development and root microbiome assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation; loss of OsBBH causes near-complete SA deficiency with benzylbenzoate accumulation and loss of benzylsalicylate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR frameshift mutants; metabolite profiling; transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding; GC-MS product identification; microsomal in vitro CYP assay; subcellular localization; chemical complementation with benzylsalicylate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling leaves; exogenous BA, benzylbenzoate or benzylsalicylate feeding; pathogen infection by Magnaporthe oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used in transient expression/localization assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene co-expression analysis using ATTED-II public RNA-seq/microarray data; pathogen-responsive expression datasets in Extended Data Fig. 10 from NCBI SRA BioProjects (specific accessions not listed in provided text)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBBH was identified as the most strongly OsCNL1-coexpressed CYP candidate. 2. OsBBH localized to the ER. 3. bbh mutants lost SA and accumulated benzylbenzoate, placing OsBBH downstream of OsBEBT. 4. Transient and microsomal assays demonstrated efficient hydroxylation of benzylbenzoate to benzylsalicylate by OsBBH. 5. Exogenous benzylsalicylate, but not benzylbenzoate, rescued SA in bbh mutants, establishing benzylbenzoate as the authentic substrate rather than BA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Resolved the longstanding search for the CYP in phenylalanine-derived SA biosynthesis and disproved the BA 2-hydroxylase model in rice
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth defects reported in bbh mutants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Specific target for enhancing pathogen-responsive SA production with likely fewer pleiotropic effects than manipulating PAL, AIM1 or ICS

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses benzylsalicylate to release salicylic acid and benzyl alcohol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the terminal product SA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant essential for SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with OsCNL1; pathogen induced as part of conserved module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen induced; co-expression with OsCNL1 supports conserved defense-associated function
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central regulator of plant immunity; also involved in abiotic stress response, development and root microbiome assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces SA; loss of OsBSE causes near-complete SA deficiency with accumulation of benzylsalicylate and benzylbenzoate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR frameshift mutants; recombinant esterase assay with purified protein; metabolite profiling; subcellular localization by confocal imaging and fractionation/immunoblot; isotope feeding; transient co-expression reconstitution with OsBBH in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling leaves; exogenous benzylbenzoate or benzylsalicylate feeding; pathogen infection by Magnaporthe oryzae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used in transient expression/localization assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene co-expression analysis using ATTED-II public RNA-seq/microarray data; pathogen-responsive expression datasets in Extended Data Fig. 10 from NCBI SRA BioProjects (specific accessions not listed in provided text)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsBSE was identified as the top OsCNL1-coexpressed putative esterase/hydrolase. 2. OsBSE localized to the cytosol. 3. Recombinant OsBSE specifically hydrolysed benzylsalicylate, not benzylbenzoate or cinnamylbenzoate. 4. bse mutants showed near-complete loss of SA and accumulation of benzylsalicylate, confirming OsBSE as the final enzyme. 5. Co-expression with OsBBH in N. benthamiana depleted benzylsalicylate and strongly increased SA, reconstituting the last step of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tobacco NtHSR203J is a close homolog whose silencing also impairs pathogen-induced SA, supporting evolutionary conservation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth defects reported in bse mutants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for specific enhancement of defense-associated SA biosynthesis with low apparent growth penalty
